text: How small can N get before the chosen method fails? Madelung-Psi should handle this well (Psi=sqrt(N+0.001)*e^{i*phi} stays bounded); direct (N, phi) almost certainly fails. Where does each method break?

motivated_by: Q1 baseline - direct (N,phi) with tight regularization should fail when min_N approaches eps_reg.

rationale: Direct method blows up rapidly: min_N goes from +1e-3 to negative within 50 steps. As soon as N drops below 0, the (sqrt(N))_xx/(2 sqrt(N)) coupling amplifies the deficit, producing max|Q|=1e5 which then back-reacts on phi_t -> u_t. Mass is preserved (continuity is exact) but local N becomes unphysical. Eps=1e-6 is too small to absorb the negative excursion driven by spectral oscillations.

motivated_by: Q1 - test if a larger regularization (matching initial background level 1e-3) buys stability.

rationale: Increasing eps_reg from 1e-6 to 1e-3 delays blowup from t=0.027 to t=0.044 (~1.6x) and reduces peak |Q| by 30x, but does NOT prevent catastrophic failure: N still goes negative and the method blows up within the first 1% of T_final=4.0. Eps_reg merely flattens Q where N is small, but the underlying dispersive instability that drives N below 0 is not cured. Conclusion: direct (N,phi) is structurally unstable for any reasonable eps_reg on this problem.

params.scheme: Strang split: L(dt/2) N(dt) L(dt/2); u via RK4 with frozen N, phi_x

motivated_by: Q1 - Madelung Psi=sqrt(N+eps)*exp(i*phi) keeps |psi|^2 >= eps so the quantum pressure term is naturally regular. Strang split puts dispersion (the source of Q) exactly in Fourier space.

rationale: Madelung-Psi with Strang split-step COMPLETES the full T=4.0 simulation. Key fact: min_N is never below -eps_mad = -1e-3, i.e. |psi|^2 = N+eps_mad >= 0 is preserved structurally (analytically guaranteed when psi is evolved unitarily; numerically we see |psi|^2 floor of zero, so N >= -eps_mad). Mass is conserved to 0.09% relative drift over T=4. Max|Q| stays bounded around 1.7e3 across the run (still large but does not run away). This is the KEY result: Madelung-Psi turns the quantum-pressure singularity into a benign factor.

text: STOP - sufficient knowledge produced. The 3 experiments establish the central failure mode: direct (N,phi) fails for any regularization on a problem with min_N ~ 1e-3, while Madelung-Psi handles it transparently. Set candidate.py to run all three for reproducibility, with Madelung-Psi marked as the best-working method.
